paper_title: CSIRO Mk3L Climate System Model Simulations

methods.description: Simulations using the CSIRO Mk3L climate system model to study Pre-Industrial and Last Glacial Maximum climates.

steps.description: The model simulations were performed using the CSIRO Mk3L climate system model version 1.2, which includes components that describe the atmosphere, land, sea ice, and ocean.
steps.searchable_summary: perform climate simulations using CSIRO Mk3L model

steps.description: Two fully coupled model experiments were undertaken to simulate the Pre-Industrial (Cpl-PI) and Last Glacial Maximum (Cpl-LGM) climates.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Cpl-PI and Cpl-LGM climate simulations

steps.description: The Cpl-PI climate was obtained by forcing the model with an atmospheric CO2 concentration of 280 ppm and by prescribing 1950 CE values for the orbital parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate Cpl-PI climate with specific CO2 and orbital parameters

steps.description: The Cpl-LGM simulation followed the protocol developed by Phase III of the Palaeoclimate Modelling Intercomparison Project (PMIP3), with specific atmospheric CO2 equivalent concentration and orbital parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate Cpl-LGM climate following PMIP3 protocol

methods.description: Simulations using Mk3L-COAL to explore the effect of different physical and biogeochemical conditions on the carbon cycle during the LGM.

steps.description: Nine additional ocean biogeochemical simulations were made with different physical and biogeochemical conditions to explore the effect on the carbon cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ocean biogeochemical simulations with Mk3L-COAL

steps.description: All experiments were forced by key boundary conditions obtained as monthly averages over the final 50 years of the fully coupled model experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: apply boundary conditions to biogeochemical simulations

steps.description: The scaling factor (S O npp) was increased by a factor of 10 to increase the export of Particulate Organic Carbon (POC) from the surface ocean.
steps.searchable_summary: increase POC export by adjusting scaling factor

steps.description: The POC remineralisation depth was increased by changing the power law exponent (b) from -0.9 to -0.7.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust POC remineralisation depth by changing power law exponent
